data.title: Senior Executive Director- Business Development
data.raw_snippet:
POSITION OBJECTIVE
 



 The Senior Executive Director – Business Development provides enterprise\-level strategic leadership to architect and execute at the forefront of institutional growth, sector expansion, and enterprise revenue performance aiming for 5X growth in 5 Years.
 



  





 The inc
data.jd_text:
POSITION OBJECTIVE
 



 The Senior Executive Director – Business Development provides enterprise\-level strategic leadership to architect and execute at the forefront of institutional growth, sector expansion, and enterprise revenue performance aiming for 5X growth in 5 Years.
 



  





 The incumbent is responsible for governing enterprise commercial architecture, sector diversification, institutional revenue sustainability, bid governance oversight, solution design alignment, pricing discipline, and commercial risk mitigation. This role ensures scalable, diversified, and margin\-accretive growth aligned with long\-term strategic objectives and institutional capability.
 



  





 This role involves cultivating strong institutional and government relationships, identifying new market opportunities, and developing comprehensive growth strategies to expand reach across sectors and geographies. The Executive Director will lead the Business Development function to ensure alignment with overall mission and long\-term strategic objectives.
 



  





 ACADEMIC QUALIFICATIONS
 



 A Master’s degree in Business Administration, Strategy, Management, or a related field is required with executive education or advanced commercial leadership qualifications being highly preferred.
 



  





 Doctoral qualification considered advantageous
 



  





 SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE
 



  





 Experience
 



 • Minimum of 15–20 years of progressive experience in Business Development, Commercial Strategy, or Enterprise Growth leadership.
 



 • Proven track record managing diversified large\-scale revenue portfolios with multi\-sector expansion.
 



 • Experience supervising Executive Director\-level leaders and commercial vertical heads.
 



 • Extensive experience in UAE institutional and government markets within multi\-cultural environments.
 



  





 Technical \& Strategic Expertise
 



 • Enterprise growth strategy execution aligned to ≥30% CAGR performance expectations.
 



 • Revenue diversification and concentration risk governance frameworks.
 



 • Pricing architecture, margin protection, and contract structuring expertise.
 



 • Pipeline forecasting governance with structured variance control discipline.
 



 • Thorough Knowledge of strategic revenue planning, enterprise forecasting, and commercial governance frameworks in post\-secondary/tertiary education and lifelong learning.
 



 • Knowledge of contract administration, negotiations, business planning and change management
 



 • Ability to analyze and interpret revenue performance, pipeline data, and margin metrics.
 



 • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills and a polished, professional presence with proven ability to build strong client relationships from the C\-level of target clients.
 



 • Exceptional presentation and bespoke solutions selling skills with proven success in with contemporary higher educational practices, learning theories, active learning and formative, authentic assessment products.
 



 • Knowledge of communication, marketing, and public relation techniques aligned to growth strategyManagement \& Leadership Skills
 



 • Enterprise growth strategy execution aligned to ≥30% CAGR performance expectations.
 



 • Revenue diversification and concentration risk governance frameworks.
 



 • Pricing architecture, margin protection, and contract structuring expertise.
 



 • Pipeline forecasting governance with structured variance control discipline
 



 • Ability to build and motivate high\-performance Business Development teams.
 



 • Strong leadership capability in managing enterprise\-level growth targets and transformation initiatives.
 



 • Previous leadership experience within rapid change or transformation in Education \& Training
 



 • Superior management and leadership skills as demonstrated by the ability to form professional relationships at all levels of government, the organization and with appropriate external stakeholders as well as the ability to work effectively with a wide range of constituencies in a diverse community

 We are seeking a senior, onsite

 **UAE** 
 . This is an embedded leadership role with
 **end\-to\-end** 
 accountability for operational excellence, governance, financial pe
data.jd_text:
**The Opportunity** 




 We are seeking a senior, onsite
 **Engagement Director** 
 to lead a large\-scale, enterprise learning operations environment in the
 **UAE** 
 . This is an embedded leadership role with
 **end\-to\-end** 
 accountability for operational excellence, governance, financial performance, and senior stakeholder partnership.
 



  





 This role is designed for a hands\-on leader who thrives at the intersection of strategy and execution. You will serve as the senior onsite leader for the program, representing Infopro Learning and providing daily leadership across integrated learning services while shaping long\-term stability, performance, and innovation within a mature enterprise environment.
 



  





 This role is best suited for a leader who enjoys being close to delivery, making day\-to\-day decisions onsite, and removing operational barriers for teams.
 



  





**Required Experience** 



* **Approximately 10–15 years of experience in L\&D consulting, outsourcing, or managed services environments**
* Proven hands\-on leadership of enterprise\-scale learning operations with significant onsite responsibility
* Experience managing complex learning engagements with operational and financial accountability
* Strong background in learning strategy and capability delivery within manufacturing, industrial, or asset\-intensive environments
* Demonstrated success leading cross\-functional teams across multiple workstreams
* Track record serving as the primary senior interface for enterprise stakeholders



  





**Education** 



* Bachelor’s degree required
* Master’s degree in Business, Organizational Development, or a related field preferred



  





**What You Will Own** 




**Onsite Leadership and Operational Oversight** 



* Provide consistent, visible onsite leadership across enterprise learning operations environments in the UAE
* Own day\-to\-day operational performance, service quality, and issue resolution across multiple integrated workstreams
* Monitor real\-time delivery metrics and intervene proactively to maintain service excellence
* Lead regular operational reviews with embedded leaders to ensure alignment, escalation, and accountability
* Build trust and confidence through strong, credible leadership presence with senior enterprise stakeholders



**Strategic Partnership and Engagement Leadership** 



* Act as the senior relationship owner for a complex learning engagement, engaging senior operational and business leaders
* Shape and execute an account strategy aligned to enterprise priorities and Infopro Learning’s regional growth objectives
* Lead quarterly performance and planning reviews focused on outcomes, value realization, and continuous improvement
* Identify opportunities for capability expansion, scope evolution, and long\-term partnership growth
* Step in directly with hands\-on advisory support across the learning lifecycle when required



**Governance, Risk, and Quality Management** 



* Execute and maintain robust governance frameworks aligned to contractual commitments and service standards
* Chair governance forums and provide transparent reporting on performance, risks, and improvement initiatives
* Enforce quality assurance, compliance, and escalation protocols across all delivery activities
* Lead risk identification and resolution for high\-impact operational or commercial issues
* Maintain clear documentation of decisions, performance metrics, and governance outcomes



**Cross\-Functional Integration** 



* Orchestrate seamless collaboration across multiple learning and operations functions
* Break down silos and foster shared accountability for enterprise outcomes
* Lead cross\-functional planning to align priorities, dependencies, and resource deployment
* Ensure a consistent, high\-quality experience across all engagement touchpoints
* Drive knowledge sharing and adoption of best practices across teams



**Financial and Commercial Accountability** 



* Own financial performance for the engagement in partnership with Infopro Learning regional leadership
* Oversee workforce planning, capacity management, and resource optimization
* Establish and track KPIs, SLAs, and financial performance metrics
* Manage scope changes, commercial discussions, and contract performance
* Balance margin optimization with service quality and stakeholder satisfaction



**Team Leadership and Development** 



* Lead and inspire a high\-performing, onsite team across multiple disciplines
* Foster a culture of ownership, collaboration, and operational excellence
* Coach and develop embedded leaders through regular one\-on\-ones and performance feedback
* Champion talent development, engagement, and retention within the team
* Build a strong shared identity as an integrated delivery organization



  





**Location \& Working Model** 




 This role is based in the UAE, with primary location in
 **Dubai or Abu Dhabi** 
 . A consistent onsite presence of 4–5 days per week at client facilities is required.
 



  





**Additional Information** 




 This role supports a dedicated client program and will be employed locally in the UAE in alignment with regional employment requirements.
 



 Job require
 



  





 If you are a hands\-on learning operations leader based in the UAE and are motivated by owning
 



 delivery, building strong teams, and leading complex enterprise engagements from the ground up, we encourage you to apply.

data.title: Executive Director, Digital Assets Risk
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Summary**
 This role could be based in UAE and Hong Kong. When you start the application process you will be presented with a drop down menu showing all countries, Please ensure that you select a country where the role is based.
   

  

* Act as the second line Subject Matter Expert for Digit
data.jd_text:
**Job Summary**
 This role could be based in UAE and Hong Kong. When you start the application process you will be presented with a drop down menu showing all countries, Please ensure that you select a country where the role is based.
   

  

* Act as the second line Subject Matter Expert for Digital Assets (DA) as an asset class including advising across all relevant Risk Types (reputational risk, traded/market, treasury/capital and liquidity, operational, technology, cyber security, and credit risk).
* Lead provision of DA risk specific expertise to continuously enhance and maintain all elements of the DA Risk Management Approach (DARMA) which sets out the approach to risk management and the control framework within which risks are managed.
* Lead assessment of material risk types that the Group may be potentially exposed to arising from direct or indirect DA activities and direct Risk Framework Ownership to such risk types.
* Lead effectiveness reviews to assess relevant first and second line understand and accept their DA risk management responsibilities.
* Review and challenge that policies and standards are being adapted or created to address DA risks and training is available to staff (on a targeted basis).
* Review and challenge the assessment of DA Risk considerations in the Group’s Risk Appetite framework.
* Monitor that the Risk Framework Owners (RFOs) propose adequate risk appetite with DA risks evaluation for consideration and approval by the Board.
* Monitor Risk Type compliance to Board approved risk appetite with DA considerations using the risk information report covering all Risk Types; and highlight significant matters to the Group Chief Risk Officer (“GCRO”), senior management and senior risk committees.
* Review infrastructure to support DA reviews, and the monitoring of exposures and actions as required.
* Lead provision of regulatory interpretation for regulations classified as “Large\-Scale” with Framework implications. This also includes working with countries to support their regulatory requirements.


**Key Responsibilities**
**Strategy**
* Influence the Group’s DA Strategy and activities, advising on alignment with specific Risk Type framework, policies, standards, risk registers and risk appetite.
* Actively engage thought leaders in the DA industries to improve risk understanding, innovation, exploration, and adoption of alternative risk models and data sources.
* Conduct regulatory interpretation and associated assessments for DA related initiatives with adherence to legal and regulatory requirements.
* Evaluate and advise on risks associated with DA projects, products and investments, including the assessment of blockchain technologies and tokenomics, to ensure robust risk mitigation strategies are in place.


**Business**
* Inform the strategic intent and collective agenda for the DA Risk Management Approach
* Influence stakeholders and DA Risk subject matter experts on thinking and approach for Digital Asset activities, including situations where the Group seeks to engage clients heavily exposed to DAs
* Advise Business on alignment of individual DA activities to the Group’s DA business strategy and execution within the risk framework and parameters.
* Direct the business to undertake appropriate evaluations with regards to DA stress scenarios with support from the Group Treasury and Group Stress Testing teams as required.
* Provide timely advice, support and challenge to enable compliance with all relevant laws and regulations and support pro\-active and pre\-emptive risk mitigation.


**Processes**
* The Candidate is responsible for discharging Policy Owner related responsibilities as defined in the ERMF:
* Ensuring that processes within the DA Risk Management central function are identified and defined by Process Owners, and ensuring the completeness and accuracy of these identified processes; and
* Reviewing, challenging, and providing oversight of the DA risk profile monitored bythe DA Risk Management Function process universe.


**People \& Talent**
* Create a culture of continuous learning and development
* Foster an open environment to stimulate creativity and develop innovative solution to supports Group’s aspiration.
* Lead through example and build the appropriate culture and values.
* Set appropriate tone and expectations from the team and work in collaboration with risk and control partners.


**Risk Management**
**For DAs Initiatives, Lead Inter\-alia**
* Oversight of development of the risk assessment approach (DARMA) for any planned business direct exposure to DAs and/or entities heavily exposed to DAs.
* Required DA risk reviews:
* Provide subject matter expertise on DA risks and their interactions to core banking Business risks (across CIB/WRB) including capital and liquidity impacts, and traded and credit risk limits
* Review and challenge enhanced DA Due Diligence reviews on Business DA clients, products, initiatives and investments to enable adherence to DARMA.
* Evaluate DA risk assessments for confirmation by existing Business authorities including evaluation of blockchains and tokens, DA key management and wallet management infrastructure, operations and partnerships with DA third parties e.g. DA custodians, exchanges, staking providers etc
* Direct and review notification requirements to relevant regulators
* Oversight and challenge for the Group’s DA Risk Profile and activities including development and implementation of appropriate infrastructure to support DA reviews, and the monitoring of exposures and actions as required.
* Direct DA considerations in Risk Appetite by relevant Risk Framework Owners.
* monitoring the approved metrics through the risk information reports on an ongoing basis and that incidents of breach are being escalated by the relevant Business and/or Risk Type to the respective Committees.


**Governance**
* Run and maintain a governance forum/committee structure to provide effective risk oversight of the DA Risk Profile across the Group.
* Conduct annual effectiveness reviews to identify improvement areas and implement necessary actions.
* Lead review and challenge of on\-going compliance with and effectiveness of the risk control environment and overall exposure and risk levels to DAs
* Monitor top and emerging risks through forward looking indicators, taking into consideration to external events which may be material.
* Review and assess management action being taken to mitigate their impact.


**Regulatory \& Business Conduct**
* Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct.
* Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct.
* Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters.


**Key stakeholders**
 Internal
   

  

 Digital Assets
   

  

* Members of the Group Management Team
* Digital Assets Centre of Excellence
* Business heads
* Group and Country Risk Framework Owners
* Digital Assets SMEs
* Group Head, Internal Audit


 External
   

  

* Home and host regulators including Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) and Financial Conduct Authority (FCA)
* The Group's external auditors


**Skills And Experience**
* Traded Risk
* Digital Assets
* Credit
* Traded Risk
* Operational and Cyber Security
* Reputational
* Compliance
* Treasury


**Qualifications**
* Strong academic background
* \>15 years of experience in banking / financial services specifically capital, liquidity risk, traded and credit risk.
* \>15 years of experience in emerging Digital technology
* \>5 years direct experience in Digital Asset technology working for a Digital Asset Service Provider
* Deep expertise (at least 5 years) of evaluating financial and non\-financial risks associated with blockchains, tokens, CBDCs, stablecoins and cryptocurrencies.
* A clear understanding of novel risks in DA and how these risks manifest within relevant Risk Types such as Reputational, Traded/Market, Treasury/Capital and Liquidity, Operational, Technology and Cyber Security Risks, and Credit Risk.
* Broad experience across diverse business models within banking and Fintechs
* Strong expertise within risk management including governance and frameworks including proven verbal and written communication skills to the Board level.
* Thought leadership in digital\-related risks (Digital\-asset risk, technology risk, ICS risk, resilience)
* Strong understanding of Digital\-assets and fintech industry trends / developments, risk implications and impacts on the financial sector
* Knowledge of key regulatory and supervisory frameworks. Ideal \- direct experience of working closely with supervisors / regulators
* Strong stakeholder management, communication and influencing skills
* Excellent interpersonal skills, multicultural awareness and sensitivity
* Programme/project management
* Experience paper writing for senior level committees.
* Creativity and appetite for continuous improvement


**About Standard Chartered**
 We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us.
   

  

 Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion.
   

  

**Together We**
* Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do
* Never settle, continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well
* Are better together, we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term


**What We Offer**
**In line with our Fair Pay Charter,** 
 we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing.
   

  

* Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance, with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations.
* Time\-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum.
* Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns.
* Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market\-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first\-aiders and all sorts of self\-help toolkits
* A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning.
* Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation, one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies \- everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential.

data.title: Head of Product & Delivery
data.raw_snippet: BlackStone eIT is seeking a strategic and results\-driven Head of Product \& Delivery to lead our product development and delivery teams. This leadership role will be responsible for overseeing the entire product lifecycle, from ideation to launch and ongoing improvement, while ensuring efficient de
data.jd_text:
BlackStone eIT is seeking a strategic and results\-driven Head of Product \& Delivery to lead our product development and delivery teams. This leadership role will be responsible for overseeing the entire product lifecycle, from ideation to launch and ongoing improvement, while ensuring efficient delivery and high\-quality outcomes. The successful candidate will align product strategy with business goals, foster collaboration across teams, and drive innovation to meet customer needs.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
* Lead and manage the product and delivery teams, providing vision, motivation, and support
* Develop and execute product strategies that align with company objectives and market demands
* Oversee project delivery processes to ensure timely, high\-quality product releases
* Collaborate with stakeholders to define product roadmaps, priorities, and success metrics
* Implement best practices in Agile and other delivery methodologies to optimize performance
* Establish and monitor KPIs to assess product and delivery team effectiveness
* Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance product quality and customer satisfaction
* Manage resource allocation and budgeting to maximize efficiency and outcomes
* Maintain strong communication across departments, fostering a collaborative and innovative culture
* \- Programme delivery across all active workstreams: executive portal, data platform, governance, AI agents, crisis management systems
* \- Sprint execution: planning, backlog management, Definition of Done, mid\-sprint check\-ins, sprint reviews with live demos
* \- External vendor coordination: tracking classification vendor milestones against your integration needs; tracking agentic platform delivery against your data readiness timeline
* \- Stakeholder management: milestone reports to senior leadership, weekly status updates, requirements conversations with internal business owners
* \- Scope discipline: deciding what gets cut when time is at risk, deferring gracefully, tracking deferred work
* \- Resource management: contractor onboarding, work package definition, daily task allocation, quality review
* \- User acceptance testing: designing and facilitating UAT with non\-technical end users and senior leadership
* \- Delivery risk management: identifying risks before they become blockers, maintaining a cleared backlog that proceeds when external dependencies are slow
* \- Office representation: authorized to represent the Data \& AI Office at all levels when technical leadership is unavailable


**Requirements**
* Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Computer Science, Engineering, or related field
* Minimum 10\+ years of experience in product management and delivery leadership roles
* Proven track record of successfully managing product portfolios and delivery teams
* Strong knowledge and experience with Agile methodologies and software development lifecycles
* Excellent leadership and people management skills with the ability to build and mentor high\-performing teams
* Exceptional strategic thinking, problem\-solving, and decision\-making abilities
* Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
* Experience with project management tools and product management platforms
* Ability to manage multiple projects and priorities in a fast\-paced environment
* Experience in the technology or software industry is highly preferred
* Required Skills \& Experience
* \- Has shipped working software to real users under a hard deadline — owned the outcome, not just managed the timeline
* \- Agile/sprint delivery in small engineering teams (2\-8 people) producing working output, not status updates
* \- Technical fluency: can read an OpenAPI spec, evaluate engineering estimates, identify scope creep, and challenge "it's complex" when it's really "I haven't started"
* \- Vendor management: has managed external delivery engagements and held vendors accountable to milestones
* \- Writing functional requirements with testable acceptance criteria
* \- UAT design and facilitation for non\-technical executives
* \- Risk identification and escalation — knows the difference between a delay and a blocker
* \- Stakeholder communication at C\-suite level — can write a one\-page status note that senior leadership reads and trusts
* \- Programme management across multiple concurrent workstreams (3\-5 simultaneous)
* \- Task tracking tools (GitHub Projects, Linear, Jira)
* \- Experience operating as a deputy or chief of staff to a technical executive
* \- Experience in government, sovereign, or institutional environments is a strong plus


**Benefits**
* Paid Time Off
* Performance Bonus
* Remote Job

data.title: Area Head of MPL - SAA
data.raw_snippet:
**About US**
 A.P. Moller \- Maersk is an integrated container logistics company working to connect and simplify its customers’ supply chains. As the global leader in shipping services, the company operates in 130 countries and employs roughly 76,000 people.
   

  

 With simple end\-to\-end offeri
data.jd_text:
**About US**
 A.P. Moller \- Maersk is an integrated container logistics company working to connect and simplify its customers’ supply chains. As the global leader in shipping services, the company operates in 130 countries and employs roughly 76,000 people.
   

  

 With simple end\-to\-end offering of products and digital services, seamless customer engagement and a superior end\-to\-end delivery network, Maersk enables its customers to trade and grow by transporting goods anywhere \- all over the world.
   

  

 As Area Head of Maersk Project Logistics \- Saudi Arabia Area, you will be accountable for operational performance and profitable growth within the Area/Cluster. Leads operations and business development teams
   

  

 to deliver service excellence, execute global standards, drive commercial opportunities, and own the P\&L for their defined scope. Ensures
   

  

 alignment between product, sales, and operations to secure customer outcomes and sustainable growth. This role reports to Regional Head of MPL \- IMEA.
   

  

**Key responsibilities:**
**Leadership \& team management**
* Leads Area/Cluster operations and business development teams (incl. managers, specialists, execution staff).
* Builds capabilities, drives performance management, and ensures a strong team culture.


**Operational execution**
* Ensures end\-to\-end execution quality, service delivery, compliance, and QHSE adherence.
* Monitors operational KPIs, identifies root causes of issues, and drives timely recovery actions.
* Oversees resource planning, workload distribution, and utilisation of tools, platforms, and processes.
* Leads implementation of new solutions, product updates, systems, and operational standards.


**Commercial \& business development**
* Drives Area/Cluster growth through opportunity qualification, pipeline support, and customer solutioning.
* Partners with Area LT and Sales to retain and grow business through improved delivery, onboarding, and customer experience.
* Leads operational and commercial input into RFPs, pricing, and customer engagement.


**Governance \& performance management**
* Owns Area/Cluster P\&L for their operational scope.
* Conducts regular performance reviews and forecasting with regional leadership.
* Ensures adherence to global standards, policies, and processes and compliance with QHSE policies.
* Drives continuous improvement, cycle\-time reduction, and operational optimisation.


**Stakeholder alignment**
* Acts as primary operational \& commercial\-enablement representative for the Area/Cluster.
* Ensures alignment across Product, Sales, CX, Finance, Procurement, and Regional Ops.
* Represents the function in escalations, customer meetings, and strategic reviews.


**Skills required:**
* Strong logistics experience
* Extensive management experience with past performance across multiple geographies
* Experience with and sustained interest in systems and process management, process improvement
* Good collaborator and communicator
* In\-depth understanding of Project Logistics delivery
* Strong analytical ability with a global, digital mindset
* Strong collaborative drive and stakeholder management skills
* Extensive supply chain experience.


 Maersk is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, and we embrace different styles of thinking. Maersk is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applicants without regard to race, colour, gender, sex, age, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, marital status, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy or parental leave, veteran status, gender identity, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with all legal requirements.
   

  

 We are happy to support your need for any adjustments during the application and hiring process. If you need special assistance or an accommodation to use our website, apply for a position, or to perform a job, please contact us by emailing accommodationrequests@maersk.com.

This leadership role is critical to ensuring the highest standards of safety and environmental management across all company operations and projects.


The Senior Director \- HSE will be responsible for developing strategic initiatives, ensuring compliance with all local and international HSE regulations, and cult
